Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Pursuant To Ccp 664.6 And For Attorney Fees And Costs; Declaration Of Susan L. Mccarthy (Motions)
Off Calendar Aug-08-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Susan McCarthy and Kendall A. Vanconas for Raeanne Westlund, Petitioner.
Wendy Krog for and with Rodney R. Westlund, Respondent (via Zoom).

Ms. Krog contested the court's tentative decision, arguing that no enforceable settlement was reached. She stated that the agreement was signed under pressure at the courthouse after hours, while sheriff's deputies were ordering the parties to vacate. She contended that the issue of whether court approval would be required was identified as an essential term, but that no agreement was ever reached on that point. She further asserted that respondent did not understand the term sheet to be binding and that other trust beneficiaries had not been given notice or an opportunity to object.

In response, Ms. McCarthy argued that all essential terms had been agreed to. She maintained that court approval was not a prerequisite for enforcing the agreement and that the beneficiaries had already received their distributions and chose not to participate in the litigation. She also emphasized that neither respondent nor his counsel objected at the time of signing.

After hearing oral argument, the Court took the matter under submission for further consideration.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition For Orders Compelling Account Of Trustee, Removing Trustee And Appointing Successor Trustee.
Off Calendar Mar-15-2023 Continued To Aug-30-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Orders: Invalidating Second And Fourth Restatements Of Trust And Setting Aside Concurrently-Executed Wills As The Product Of Undue
Off Calendar Mar-15-2023 Continued To Aug-30-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kendall Vanconas and Susan McCarthy appeared by zoom for Raeanne Westlund (petitioner).
Thornton Kontz for and with Rod Westlund (both in person).
Lisa McMillan (reporter) appeared by zoom.

Mrs. Vanconas stated that they filed the second petition because after mediation, this was one issue that was left hanging out there. Mr. Kontz stated that they were delayed in providing the accounting, but they finally did it. Discussion was held regarding the start date of the accounting, whether it is appropriate to request that an accounting be provided since 2004, the issue of when the trustor was considered to be incompetent, the trustee agreeing not to take a trustee fee, the need to do depositions, the parties having done JAMS and not being able to settle, the period the trustee is obligated to account for, the issue of conflict of interest, the issue of over $70,000.00 in trust assets having been spent in litigation, the issue of litigation not being for the benefit of the trust, Mr. Westlund not acting impartially, the issue of surcharge, the issue of how and when the fourth amendment came about, the issue of whether or not the trustee has a right to defend the trust, etc. The court raised the issue of having a case management session and suggested that counsel ask for a litigation budget. The Petition for Orders Invalidating Second and Fourth Restatements of Trust, etc., and the Petition for Orders Compelling Account of Trustee, etc. are continued to August 30, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. A case management hearing is also set for that date. A joint case management statement is to be filed by August 21. 2023. The court prefers that the parties meet and confer regarding additional time for the accounting.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Choi. Reporter: Lisa McMillan, CSR #10383, 831-234-6135, lisa3377@comcast.net

Petition For Orders: Invalidating Second And Fourth Restatements Of Trust And Setting Aside Concurrently-Executed Wills As The Product Of Undue
Off Calendar Aug-03-2022 Continued To Nov-09-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kendall VanConas and Susan McCarthy, via Zoom, for Raeanne Westlund; Thornton Kontz, via Zoom, for Rodney Westlund, Respondent.

The court commends counsel for the filing of a detailed Joint Case Management Statement. The court and counsel discuss the status of the case with the court pointing to Petitioner's intent to file a Motion to Remove Trustee, pending discovery and the suggestion that the parties may be ready for trial in April of 2023. Mr. Kontz reports that he intends to forward Ms. VanConas an accounting by Oct-01-2022.

After additional discussion, the matter is continued to Nov-09-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. The Case Management Hearing is taken off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

Petition For Orders: Invalidating Second And Fourth Restatements Of Trust And Setting Aside Concurrently-Executed Wills As The Product Of Undue
Off Calendar Jun-22-2022 Continued To Aug-03-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Susan McCarthy, via Zoom, for Raeanne Westlund, Petitioner; Thornton Kontz, via Zoom, for Rodney Westlund, Respondent.

The court confirms with counsel that this matter did not settle with Ret. Judge John H. Sugiyama. The court advises that it will be expecting a new joint Case Management Statement ("CMS") and asks counsel for their respective positions. Mr. Kontz reports that his position has not really changed and will be requesting an evidentiary hearing. Ms. McCarthy agrees with Mr. Kontz and adds that Trustee Rodney Westlund has not provided a formal accounting and as both a Trustee and Beneficiary, would like to see him removed as Trustee and to appoint a third party to act in that capacity. Mr. Kontz reports that he would have no issue providing a full, statutory accounting within a few months and states that an evidentiary hearing should be set regarding removing his client as Trustee. The court reiterates that they will be required to file a joint CMS which should include proposed trial dates.

The matter is continued to Aug-03-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. A Case Management Hearing is set for the same date. Counsel is instructed to file a joint Case Management Statement on or before Jul-20-2022.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Bastain. Not Reported.

Off Calendar Oct-25-2021 Continued To Feb-14-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thornton Kontz, via ZOOM, for Rodney Westlund, Respondent; Susan McCarthy and Kendall VanConas, via CourtCall, for Raeanne Westlund.

Mr. Kontz reports that he has proposed using a private mediator to assist with settlement discussions. Ms. McCarthy and Ms. VanConas confirm that they are amenable to Mr. Kontz' proposal.

The Petition for Orders, etc., is continued to Feb-14-2022 at 9:00 am in Department 204. A Status of Mediation is set for the same date. The parties are instructed to file a joint statement regarding the status of mediation by Feb-07-2022. The Case Management Hearing is off calendar.

Petition For Orders: Invalidating Second And Fourth Restatements Of Trust And Setting Aside Concurrently-Executed Wills As The Product Of Undue
Off Calendar Jul-19-2021 Continued To Oct-25-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kendall Vanconas and Susan McCarthy, via CourtCall, for Raeanne Westlund, Petitioner; Thornton Kontz, via ZOOM, for Rodney Westlund, Respondent.

At the outset, the court asks counsel for a status regarding settlement discussions. Ms. Vaconas and Ms. McCarthy report that discovery has been served. Mr. Kontz believes that depositions should be calendared within the next few months. The court queries counsel regarding interest in pro bono mediation. Counsel advises that they would be amenable to mediation but would like to complete discovery first. The court advises counsel that a Case Management hearing will be set for the next calling of this case and an order will be issued by the court, accordingly.

After additional discussion, the Petition for Orders, etc., is continued to October 25, 2021, at 9:00 am in Department 204. A Case Management hearing is set for the same date. A joint Case Management Statement should be filed with the court by no later than October 12, 2021.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: B. Quesada. Not reported.

Petition For Orders: Invalidating Second And Fourth Restatements Of Trust And Setting Aside Concurrently-Executed Wills As The Product Of Undue
Off Calendar Mar-15-2021 Continued To Jul-19-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Susan McCarthy appeared by telephone for Raeanne Westlund.
Thornton Kontz appeared by zoom for Rod Westlund (Respondent).

The Court asked where things stand. Counsel reported that some written discovery has been propounded, that some responses have been received, that some information is still missing, that some third-party subpoenas have been issued and there are still some that need to be issued, that they still have a lot of discovery to do, etc. Ms. McCarthy stated that she doesn't feel they have enough information to go to mediation at this time. Mr. Kontz stated that he has suggested proceeding with someone from JAMS in about 90 to 120-days and that he believes the trust can afford it. The Court raised the issue of appointing a neutral trustee. Mr. Kontz responded "no". Ms. McCarthy responded "yes". Ms. McCarthy also stated that the accounting that was provided was incomplete and that they have asked for information, but received no response. The Court suggested that Ms. McCarthy speak with Mr. Kontz. The Petition for Orders Invalidating Second and Fourth Restatements of Trust, etc. is continued to July 19, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.
